gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/256)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (20/256)
text: لَا بَأْسَ بِالرَّجُلِ يَتَمَتَّعُ بِالْمَرْأَةِ عَلَى حُكْمِهِ وَ لَكِنْ لَا بُدَّ لَهُ مِنْ أَنْ يُعْطِيَهَا شَيْئاً لِأَنَّهُ إِنْ أُحْدِثَ بِهِ حَدَثٌ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهَا مِيرَاثٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, form Ibn Abu Umehyr, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘There is no problem with the man who marries by Mut’a with the woman upon his decision, but there is no alternative for him from giving her something, because if there was to occur an event with him (of death), there would not happen to be any inheritance for her’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain persons of our people who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It is not unlawful for a man to marry a woman in the manner of al-Mut‘ah (advantageous marriage) because of leaving herself at his disposal, but it is necessary to give her something; if anything happens to him she will not inherit his legacy.’”
